NVL.notes: NVL(a,b) -> COALESCE(a,b). Oracle treats '' as NULL; Spark does not.

NVL2.spark_sql: CASE WHEN {0} IS NOT NULL THEN {1} ELSE {2} END

DECODE.notes: DECODE(expr, search1, result1, ..., default) -> CASE WHEN expr=search1 THEN result1 ... ELSE default END

SYSDATE.notes: Oracle SYSDATE has second precision. Spark CURRENT_TIMESTAMP has microsecond precision.

TRUNC_DATE.notes: Oracle TRUNC(date) truncates to day. TRUNC(date,'MONTH') to first of month. date - N subtracts N days in Oracle.

TO_CHAR.notes: Oracle TO_CHAR(date, fmt) -> Spark DATE_FORMAT(date, fmt). TO_CHAR(number) -> CAST(number AS STRING).

TO_DATE.notes: Oracle TO_DATE(str, fmt) -> Spark TO_DATE(str, fmt) with format mapping applied. Oracle implicit string-to-date (e.g., WHERE date >= '01-Mar-20') must become explicit TO_DATE.

TO_NUMBER.notes: Oracle TO_NUMBER(str) -> CAST(str AS DECIMAL) or CAST(str AS INT/BIGINT) depending on context.

ADD_MONTHS.notes: Same function name in Spark SQL. Semantics are compatible.

MONTHS_BETWEEN.notes: Same function name. Spark returns a double; Oracle returns a number.

LAST_DAY.notes: Same function name in Spark SQL.

INSTR.notes: Oracle INSTR(str, substr, pos, occurrence) — Spark INSTR only supports (str, substr). For pos/occurrence, use LOCATE or regexp.

SUBSTR.notes: Oracle SUBSTR(str, pos, len) -> Spark SUBSTRING(str, pos, len). 1-based indexing in both.

REPLACE.notes: Same function name for simple replacement.

ROUND.notes: Same function name. Banker's rounding difference possible.

LEAD.notes: Same function name. Requires OVER() clause.

LAG.notes: Same function name. Requires OVER() clause.

ROW_NUMBER.notes: Same function name. Requires OVER() clause.

ROWNUM.notes: Oracle ROWNUM is a pseudo-column. In WHERE: ROWNUM < N -> use ROW_NUMBER() window or LIMIT. In correlated subquery with ROWNUM < 2: means 'first matching row'.

DUAL.notes: Oracle's DUAL table. In Spark SQL, just remove 'FROM DUAL'. SELECT 1 FROM DUAL -> SELECT 1.

SQLERRM.notes: Oracle SQLERRM returns error message string. In Python: except Exception as e: str(e)

COMMIT.notes: No direct Spark equivalent. Represents write materialization point. In PySpark, each write operation is atomic.

ROLLBACK.notes: No direct Spark equivalent. Use try/except in Python for error handling.

DBMS_STATS.Gather_Table_Stats.notes: Optional in Spark. Spark handles stats automatically for most operations.

DBMS_SCHEDULER.Create_Job.notes: Replace with Python concurrent execution or Airflow DAG.

EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'truncate table'.notes: Or use DataFrame write with mode='overwrite'.

EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'alter table'.replacement: # Partition management handled by Spark/Iceberg automatically
EXECUTE IMMEDIATE 'alter table'.notes: Spark/Iceberg handles partitioning differently. ADD PARTITION, index rebuild not needed.

outer_join_syntax.WHERE a.col = b.col(+): LEFT JOIN b ON a.col = b.col
outer_join_syntax.WHERE a.col(+) = b.col: RIGHT JOIN b ON a.col = b.col
outer_join_syntax.notes: Oracle (+) outer join syntax. The (+) goes on the deficient side.

implicit_conversions.string_to_date: Oracle implicitly converts strings to dates using NLS_DATE_FORMAT. Spark requires explicit TO_DATE() with format string.
implicit_conversions.null_empty_string: Oracle treats '' (empty string) as NULL. Spark does not. This affects NVL/COALESCE behavior on string columns.
implicit_conversions.number_to_string: Oracle implicitly converts numbers to strings in concatenation. Spark requires explicit CAST.
